notes:
1. I usually can't complete a whole match without a crash - usually get about 10-15 minutes in before it happens (This is offline. I haven't tested online yet). Seems to last longer without crashing in 1vs1 matches though. Crashes are much less likely when just browsing menus. I also noticed that in some situations you wouldn't hear characters speaking and would have to rely on the subtitles.
---
2. As for performance, I haven't played it on Windows to compare but it seems to run pretty good if I keep it at 1080p, staying at or close to my 60fps vsync cap. It can slow down a bit if there's lots of characters on screen at once (8 man Hell in a Cell makes it chug a bit, for example).
---
3. Disabling Esync apparently makes no difference in terms of stability. YMMV.
---
4.Over all, it's pretty playable but it CTD's or locks up my system a bit too often to give it a better rating. I will add another report if I see improvements.
title: WWE 2K19

notes:
Was working before hand. After reinstalling windows a while back trying it out, i decided that I was still unhappy with it. Reinstalling Pop OS I have invisible wrestlers and I'm not sure why (fresh install).  Tried new saves and deleting the config files in the compat data nothing which is a shame as the  performance is perfect once shaders are cached.  Waiting on an update to fix the issue.

Note: Upgraded to V5 kernel to fix a mesa sleep bug, the default kernel still had this issue.
title: WWE 2K19
